dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to detect new multi-day extremes. Entry long: price tests new high then closes lower (reversal). Entry short: price tests new low then closes higher (reversal). Small body_pct indicates reversal exhaustion. Exit conditions trigger on continuation through previous bar extremes. Stop loss set at 2.5x ATR equivalent (2.5%) as specified. Hold max 5 bars per signal specification. Gap closure percentage (50% minimum) cannot be directly encoded without lookback indexing, so reversal structure detection via body_pct and close positioning against prev_close/prev_high/prev_low serves as proxy.

backtest_rule.reason: Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to detect new multi-day extremes. Entry long: price tests new high then closes lower (reversal). Entry short: price tests new low then closes higher (reversal). Small body_pct indicates reversal exhaustion. Exit conditions trigger on continuation through previous bar extremes. Stop loss set at 2.5x ATR equivalent (2.5%) as specified. Hold max 5 bars per signal specification. Gap closure percentage (50% minimum) cannot be directly encoded without lookback indexing, so reversal structure detection via body_pct and close positioning against prev_close/prev_high/prev_low serves as proxy.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter a trade expecting a reversal when the market tests a new extreme, then fails that test by closing at the opposite end of the range or showing weakness on a subsequent bar, signaling exhaustion of the trend.
